content:
type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Same 2 files needed by both features, can't be fully disjoint. Propose functional split instead: I own compression logic only (compress param in Cache.__init__, _encode/_decode helpers w/ DSPC magic header, put/get compression branches, configure_cache's new compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params). You own TTL logic only (ttl params in Cache.__init__, expiry checks, configure_cache's new ttl params). We each add our own new params to the existing signatures without touching each other's lines, commit separately, then merge via git at the end. I'll git merge team/agent1 before writing patch.txt. Sound good?
